name: Granite
description_short: Granite Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Salt Lake County, Utah, offering competitive rates on mortgages, auto loans, and deposit accounts with innovative products like Dividend+ Checking and Vanishing Rate Auto Loans.
description_long:
Granite Credit Union is a federally insured credit union (NCUA) headquartered in Millcreek, Utah, serving the Salt Lake County area. As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution, it operates under the credit union model where members become part-owners and benefit from the organization's earnings through competitive rates and lower fees compared to traditional banks.

Granite offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including mortgages (home purchase and construction loans), home equity lines of credit (HELOCs), home equity loans, auto loans, credit cards (Platinum Visa with Cashback rewards), checking accounts (Dividend+ Spend Account), savings accounts, and certificates of deposit. The credit union advertises differentiated products such as a Vanishing Rate Auto Loan that reduces the APR by 0.05% for every three consecutive on-time payments, and a Dividend+ Checking account that earns dividends. They also promote mortgage seminars and financial education to members.

Granite distinguishes itself through product innovation and local community focus. The Vanishing Rate Auto Loan is a unique incentive-based product that rewards payment behavior, and the Dividend+ Checking account extends dividend-earning benefits beyond traditional savings products. The institution maintains multiple physical branch locations, shares in the CO-OP ATM network for fee-free access, and operates shared branching services. They emphasize personalized service with local mortgage specialists and live chat support, positioning themselves as a community-driven alternative to larger financial institutions.

While Granite offers competitive products and personalized service within their geographic footprint, membership is restricted to Salt Lake County residents and those who work in the area, limiting accessibility for broader populations. Current advertised rates are moderate but not exceptional (auto loans at 4.74% APR, savings at 0.05% APY, HELOCs at 7.25% variable), and rates vary by creditworthiness. As a smaller regional institution, they may have fewer products and technology features compared to larger national credit unions or banks.
services: Auto loans with Vanishing Rate feature (APR reduces 0.05% per 3 on-time payments)
Home mortgages including purchase and refinance loans
Home Equity Lines of Credit (HELOC) with 3.99% introductory rate
Home equity loans at fixed rates
Platinum Visa credit card with variable APR
Cashback Visa credit card with 1.5% rewards
Dividend+ Spend Checking account with dividend earnings
Mobile banking app and online account management
pros: Vanishing Rate Auto Loan feature reduces APR by 0.05% for every 3 consecutive on-time payments, rewarding payment behavior
Dividend+ Checking account earns dividends, extending income-generation beyond savings accounts
Cashback Visa credit card offers 1.5% cash back on all purchases
Access to CO-OP shared branching network and fee-free ATMs beyond owned locations
HELOC introductory rate of 3.99% for 6 months (variable 7.25% thereafter) for home equity access
Residential construction loans available starting at 6.25%
Local personalized service with mortgage seminars and financial education programs
cons: Membership limited to Salt Lake County residents or those who work in the area, restricting access
Savings account APY of 0.05% is extremely low compared to high-yield online savings alternatives
Auto loan APR of 4.74% and credit card APR of 11.49% are moderate but not competitively low in current market
Limited geographic footprint with only a handful of physical branches reduces convenience outside immediate service area
No mention of business lending, investment services, or advanced digital banking features on primary website
best_for: Salt Lake County residents seeking personalized mortgage and home equity lending with local branch support
Auto buyers who can maintain consistent on-time payments and benefit from the Vanishing Rate incentive structure
Homeowners interested in HELOCs with introductory rate offers for renovation or consolidation purposes
Members prioritizing community-based credit union values and dividend returns over largest national institutions
diagnosis: Granite Credit Union is best for Salt Lake County-area residents seeking personalized mortgage, home equity, and auto lending with community-focused service and member-owned benefits. The primary caveat is geographic membership restriction limiting access to those living or working in Salt Lake County, and competitive rates that are moderate rather than exceptional in current market conditions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Granite — Credit Union in SALT LAKE CITY, UT | CreditDoc
meta_description: Granite — NCUA-insured credit union in SALT LAKE CITY, UT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
